agents.name: grimoire.typescript-coder
agents.description: Use this agent when the user needs to write, refactor, debug, review, or understand TypeScript code in any environment — Node.js backends, React, Angular, Vue, Svelte, or any other TypeScript-compatible platform. This includes tasks like designing type-safe data models, implementing utility types, handling errors with discriminated unions, refactoring JavaScript to TypeScript, or reviewing TypeScript code for type safety and correctness.

agents.name: grimoire.typescript-coder-guided
agents.description: Writes, refactors, and debugs TypeScript code in any environment — Node.js backends, React, Angular, Vue, Svelte. Guided variant: treats explicit direction as binding — flags conflicts with best practice and recommends alternatives, but implements what was asked (refusing only genuinely harmful directions).

agents.name: grimoire.typescript-coder-opinionated
agents.description: Writes, refactors, and debugs TypeScript code in any environment — Node.js backends, React, Angular, Vue, Svelte. Opinionated variant: prioritizes engineering quality over instructions — implements the best-practice approach and documents every deviation with its rationale.

skills.name: grimoire.modern-typescript
skills.description: Modern TypeScript best practices, patterns, and type system mastery for TS 5.7+. Use when writing TypeScript, reviewing TS code, designing types, configuring tsconfig, or asking about TypeScript patterns, generics, type safety, strict mode, idiomatic TypeScript, discriminated unions, branded types, or modern TS features.

skills.name: grimoire.unit-testing-typescript
skills.description: Framework selection, patterns, and best practices for TypeScript/JavaScript unit testing with Vitest, Jest, Mocha, and Node test runner. Use when writing tests for .ts/.tsx/.js files, configuring test frameworks, or asking about TypeScript testing patterns, mocking, assertions, async testing.
